questions: Should `setLightboxAssetKey` be exposed on the ops context for potential external use (e.g., opening lightbox programmatically from other components), or should only `onDoubleClickAsset` be public? The brief says expose it, but it means any consumer could open the lightbox bypassing the generationId guard.
The `useRef` import in VideoEditorProvider — is it used for anything else besides `lightboxHandlerRef`? If so we keep it; if not we remove it. (I'll check during implementation, but flagging.)

success_criteria.criterion: Double-clicking an asset in standard (non-condensed) layout opens the MediaLightbox
Double-clicking an asset in condensed layout opens the MediaLightbox
Closing the lightbox resets state — no stale lightbox on layout switch
registerLightboxHandler and lightboxHandlerRef are fully removed from codebase
Lightbox state, query, and MediaLightbox rendering are removed from PreviewPanel
No duplicate lightbox rendering — only one MediaLightbox instance exists in the tree
PreviewPanel.tsx shrinks by ~30 lines from removing lightbox code

assumptions: The `useRef` in VideoEditorProvider.tsx is only used for `lightboxHandlerRef` — if wrong, we keep the import.
No other components besides PreviewPanel and CondensedLightboxProvider call `registerLightboxHandler` (grep confirms this).
The lightbox query key can be unified to `['video-editor', 'lightbox', generationId]` — the split between 'preview-lightbox' and 'condensed-lightbox' was incidental and they can share cache.
OverlayEditor's `onDoubleClickAsset` prop interface stays the same — PreviewPanel just passes through the context function instead of a local closure.

We will expose `setLightboxAssetKey` on the ops context per the brief, even though `onDoubleClickAsset` already wraps it with validation. This allows OverlayEditor (via PreviewPanel) to use `onDoubleClickAsset` directly rather than needing its own validation.
